summary: Iteration 11 WebRTC refactor — VOICE ROOM AUDIO MESH + 1:1 CALL REGRESSION all passing. Two independent chromium contexts (channel='chromium' + --use-fake-ui-for-media-devices + --use-fake-device-for-media-stream + permissions=['microphone']). Mei (VIP) hosts a fresh room, demo joins; both render room-screen with host card and '2 members'; host has room-mic-btn, listener has room-hand-btn. Mic off/on toggle works with no crash. demo raises hand → host sees hand-requests-panel. demo leaves → host still stable → host ends room cleanly. Console scan across both contexts: 0 addIceCandidate/setRemoteDescription/InvalidStateError/OperationError messages, 0 uncaught pageerrors, no rtc_offer flood. 1:1 regression (demo → mei from chat): outgoing overlay shows, mei's page receives incoming, accept → both sides show call-timer, end closes both overlays. Shared getRTC/webrtcAvailable abstraction and per-peer ICE buffering are working as designed.
critical_code_review_comments: use-room-audio.ts flushIce is guarded by pc.remoteDescription — correct ordering; queued candidates flushed on offer, answer, and every rtc_ice event.
Deterministic initiator (myId > peerId) prevents glare; onconnectionstatechange==='failed' triggers closePeer + re-offer only from initiator, avoiding double reconnect.
webrtc.ts gracefully returns null on Expo Go (no react-native-webrtc) — useRoomAudio/CallContext both early-return via webrtcAvailable().
Room ICE messages carry room_id and are gated by event.room_id !== roomId in the subscribe handler — no cross-room leakage.
seed_data_creation: One live voice room created per run (title 'WebRTC Regression <ts>') and immediately ended by the host — cleaned up automatically.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Playwright MUST use channel='chromium' (not headless_shell) plus --use-fake-ui-for-media-devices, --use-fake-device-for-media-stream, and context permissions=['microphone'] — otherwise getUserMedia rejects and the room mounts without local tracks. Language chips inside the create-room modal live inside a horizontal ScrollView that clips off-screen chips; use element.evaluate('el => el.click()') as a fallback when Playwright reports 'outside of the viewport'. Test file: /app/test_reports/room_audio_test.py.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no regressions found. The webrtc.ts extraction and use-room-audio.ts hardening (ICE buffering per-peer, re-offer on 'failed', proper cleanup) are functioning cleanly on web; native path is code-reviewed but not runtime-testable in Expo Go / web preview.
